title: diplazium subsinuatum f. simplicifolium
intro: Diplazium subsinuatum f. simplicifolium, also known as the compound-leaved umbrella fern, is a type of fern that belongs to the family Athyriaceae. It is native to China and is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The plant is used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its health benefits of reducing inflammation, improving immune function, and promoting overall well-being. Herbal preparations of Diplazium subsinuatum f. simplicifolium include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health problems.
